wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the VFloat project overview section. Explain the library's purpose as a Vue 3 floating UI positioning solution built on @floating-ui/dom. Document the core architecture, key features including precise positioning, Vue 3 composables, interaction handling, and nested element support. Include the project status as work-in-progress with rapid evolution and potential breaking changes. Provide installation instructions via pnpm, npm, and yarn. Summarize the TypeScript support, cross-platform compatibility, and browser support. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and highlight the relationship to Floating UI and VueUse dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Built-in Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the built-in middleware collection covering all positioning modifiers available in the VFloat library. Document the offset middleware for adding spacing around floating elements, including distance and main/start/end configurations. Explain the flip middleware for automatic collision avoidance and placement switching. Detail the shift middleware for viewport boundary adjustments and overflow handling. Cover the hide middleware for visibility management based on clipping detection. Document the size middleware for dynamic element sizing and measurement. Include the arrow middleware with padding support and element reference configuration. Provide practical examples of each middleware usage, configuration options, and common parameter combinations. Address performance considerations and best practices for middleware selection and ordering.

wiki_catalogs.name: useFloating Composable
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the useFloating composable, the core positioning engine of VFloat. Explain its role as the central orchestrator for floating element positioning with middleware support. Document the complete API including parameters (anchorEl, floatingEl, options), return values (FloatingContext), and configuration options (placement, strategy, transform, middlewares, autoUpdate, open, onOpenChange). Detail the positioning algorithms, collision detection, viewport boundary handling, and automatic update mechanisms. Explain the FloatingContext object structure including x/y coordinates, placement, strategy, middlewareData, isPositioned, floatingStyles, update method, refs object, open state, and setOpen function. Cover device pixel ratio awareness, transform vs top/left positioning modes, and reactive CSS styles. Include practical examples demonstrating different positioning strategies, middleware combinations, and integration patterns with interaction composables.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hover Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the hover interaction composable. Document the useHover function's API including delay configuration for both open and close actions, rest detection mechanism for pointer rest time, mouse-only mode for excluding touch devices, and safe polygon algorithm for smooth pointer navigation. Explain the delayed open/close functionality with timeout management, pointer event handling for enter/leave scenarios, and integration with the positioning system. Include practical examples showing tooltip creation, dropdown hover behavior, and custom delay configurations. Document the safe polygon algorithm implementation, buffer options, and visual polygon debugging features. Address cross-device compatibility, performance considerations, and accessibility implications for hover interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Floating Tree System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the floating tree system, the advanced hierarchical positioning capability of VFloat. Document the useFloatingTree composable and its role in managing complex nested floating element structures. Explain tree node coordination, parent-child relationships, and mutually exclusive submenu behavior. Detail the floating tree API including node registration, tree context management, and event propagation. Cover practical implementation patterns for nested menus, context menus, and complex UI hierarchies. Include performance considerations for large tree structures, memory management, and cleanup strategies. Provide step-by-step tutorials for building multi-level dropdowns, accordion menus, and interactive navigation systems. Document integration with other VFloat composables and common pitfalls to avoid.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for VFloat. Begin with installation instructions using pnpm, npm, and yarn. Provide step-by-step setup guide for Vue 3 projects. Include the three fundamental examples from the README: basic tooltip, dropdown menu, and tooltip with arrow. Explain the core concepts of anchor elements, floating elements, and context objects. Demonstrate the use of useFloating composable with placement options and middlewares. Show how to combine positioning with interaction composables like useHover and useClick. Include basic styling examples and explain the floatingStyles reactive object. Address common beginner mistakes and provide troubleshooting tips for initial setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Middleware Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for creating custom middleware in the VFloat positioning system. Explain the middleware interface structure including the name, options, and fn function signature. Document the middleware execution pipeline and how data flows between stages. Cover the MiddlewareArgs interface and how to access floating context, elements, and positioning data. Detail the MiddlewareReturn object structure and how to modify positioning calculations. Include practical examples of custom middleware development from simple transformations to complex positioning logic. Explain data sharing patterns between middleware stages and how to pass computed values. Address performance optimization techniques, memoization strategies, and avoiding unnecessary recalculations. Document testing approaches for custom middleware and integration patterns with existing middleware chains.

wiki_catalogs.name: useArrow Composable
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the useArrow composable and middleware system. Explain how the arrow positioning works in conjunction with useFloating to create tooltips and popovers with directional arrows. Document the arrow middleware configuration including element reference, padding options, and automatic arrow positioning. Detail the integration with useFloating's middleware pipeline and how arrow positioning data flows through the positioning system. Explain the arrow element requirements, styling considerations, and positioning calculations. Cover the arrow middleware interface, data sharing with other middleware, and performance considerations. Include practical examples showing tooltip creation with arrows, custom arrow positioning, and integration with different placement strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Click Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the click interaction composable. Document the useClick function's API including toggle behavior for showing/hiding floating elements, outside click detection for automatic closing, pointer type filtering for excluding certain input methods, and drag event handling prevention. Explain the click-to-open/close logic, event delegation patterns, and integration with the positioning system. Include practical examples showing dropdown menus, context menus, and modal toggling. Document the outside click detection algorithm, pointer type validation, and edge case handling for different input devices. Address accessibility considerations for keyboard navigation alongside mouse clicks, performance optimization for event listeners, and common integration patterns with forms and interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Virtual Elements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for virtual elements, a powerful VFloat feature enabling flexible positioning logic beyond traditional anchor/floating element pairs. Explain the VirtualElement interface and its implementation for custom positioning scenarios. Document use cases including context menus, tooltip positioning relative to mouse coordinates, and dynamic element placement. Detail the virtual element lifecycle, update mechanisms, and integration with the positioning pipeline. Cover advanced patterns for creating custom positioning logic, handling dynamic content, and optimizing performance. Include practical examples of implementing context menus, cursor-following tooltips, and responsive positioning systems. Address common challenges and best practices for virtual element implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Positioning System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core positioning system covering the fundamental positioning capabilities of VFloat. Document the useFloating composable as the central orchestrator, explaining its role in managing floating element positioning with middleware support. Detail the positioning algorithms, collision detection, and viewport boundary handling. Explain the useArrow composable for arrow element positioning with offset configuration and padding support. Cover useClientPoint for cursor/touch coordinate-based positioning with axis-specific positioning options. Document the FloatingContext object structure, floatingStyles reactive CSS properties, and device pixel ratio awareness. Include configuration options, return values, and practical examples demonstrating different positioning strategies and middleware combinations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the middleware pipeline architecture and execution model in VFloat. Explain how middleware functions are chained together and executed sequentially in the positioning pipeline. Document the execution order importance and how middleware interactions affect final positioning results. Cover the MiddlewareData interface and how data is shared between pipeline stages. Detail the fn function signature and how middleware can modify positioning calculations. Explain the relationship between middleware and the core positioning engine. Include examples of optimal middleware ordering for common use cases like tooltips with arrows, dropdown menus with collision detection, and complex floating element hierarchies. Address debugging techniques for middleware chains and performance profiling of positioning pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: useClientPoint Composable
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the useClientPoint composable for cursor and touch coordinate-based positioning. Explain how this composable enables positioning floating elements relative to mouse cursor or touch coordinates. Document the coordinate system, axis-specific positioning options, and integration with useFloating for dynamic positioning updates. Detail the client point calculation methods, touch event handling, and pointer type filtering. Explain the relationship between client coordinates and element positioning, including viewport boundary considerations and coordinate transformations. Cover practical use cases such as context menus, custom tooltips, and interactive positioning elements. Include examples demonstrating cursor-following elements, touch-based positioning, and integration with hover and click interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Keyboard Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for keyboard interaction composables. Document useFocus for keyboard focus management including focus detection, blur handling, and integration with the positioning system. Cover useEscapeKey for escape key handling enabling modal dismissal and programmatic closing. Explain focus management patterns for accessible interfaces, keyboard navigation workflows, and event handling for different key combinations. Include practical examples showing keyboard-triggered tooltips, modal escape handling, and focus restoration patterns. Document accessibility compliance with ARIA standards, screen reader compatibility, and keyboard-only navigation support. Address focus trapping considerations, tab order management, and integration with form elements and interactive components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Safe Polygon Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the safe polygon algorithm used in VFloat's hover interactions. Explain the mathematical foundation of the polygon algorithm for smooth pointer navigation between elements. Document how the algorithm prevents accidental triggering of hover states when moving between related floating elements. Detail the polygon calculation process, vertex definition, and collision detection mechanisms. Cover configuration options for polygon padding, sensitivity adjustments, and performance tuning. Include visual examples and diagrams illustrating polygon shapes and hover behavior. Provide implementation guidance for custom hover interactions, touch device considerations, and accessibility compliance. Address common issues with polygon geometry and optimization strategies for complex layouts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interaction System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the interaction system covering all user interaction composables in VFloat. Document useHover with delay mechanisms, safe polygon algorithm, and mouse-only mode. Explain useClick for toggle behavior, outside click detection, and pointer type filtering. Detail useFocus and useEscapeKey for keyboard navigation and modal dismissal. Cover useListNavigation for keyboard navigation in lists with arrow key handling. Document useFocusTrap for accessibility-compliant focus management in modals. Include configuration options, event handling patterns, and integration examples showing how interactions work with the positioning system. Address accessibility considerations, cross-device compatibility, and common interaction patterns for tooltips, dropdowns, and menus.

wiki_catalogs.name: Virtual Elements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for virtual elements concept and implementation in VFloat. Explain how virtual elements enable flexible positioning logic without requiring actual DOM elements. Document the VirtualElement interface including getBoundingClientRect method, context properties, and event handling capabilities. Detail use cases such as context-based positioning, programmatic positioning, and custom anchor logic. Explain the relationship between virtual elements and traditional DOM elements, conversion patterns, and integration with useFloating. Cover advanced scenarios like dynamic positioning, conditional anchoring, and custom positioning algorithms. Include practical examples demonstrating virtual element creation, context menu positioning, and custom anchor implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: List Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the list navigation composable. Document the useListNavigation function's API including arrow key handling for up/down navigation, home/end key support, page up/page down functionality, and character search for fast navigation. Explain focus management within list structures, boundary handling for navigation loops, and integration with the positioning system for dropdown menus and select components. Include practical examples showing searchable dropdowns, keyboard-navigable menus, and accessible list interfaces. Document the navigation algorithm, focus restoration patterns, and performance considerations for large lists. Address accessibility requirements for keyboard navigation, screen reader announcements, and integration with virtual scrolling techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Device Pixel Ratio Awareness
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document VFloat's device pixel ratio (DPR) awareness and its impact on positioning accuracy and visual quality. Explain how VFloat calculates and applies device pixel ratios to ensure crisp positioning on high-DPI displays. Detail the DPR calculation methodology, rounding strategies, and performance implications. Cover the relationship between device pixel ratio and positioning precision, including how DPR affects coordinate calculations and element positioning. Document configuration options for controlling DPR behavior and performance trade-offs. Include examples of positioning accuracy improvements on Retina displays and other high-DPI devices. Address common issues with DPR handling and best practices for maintaining visual quality across different display densities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the middleware system covering the pluggable positioning modifier architecture. Document the middleware pipeline concept and how middleware functions transform positioning data sequentially. Explain the built-in middleware including offset for spacing, flip for collision avoidance, shift for viewport adjustment, hide for visibility management, autoPlacement for optimal placement selection, and size for dynamic sizing. Detail the useArrow middleware specifically for arrow positioning with padding support. Cover custom middleware development including the middleware interface, data sharing between stages, and performance optimization. Include practical examples of middleware combinations, execution order importance, and best practices for creating efficient positioning pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Focus Trap
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the focus trap composable. Document the useFocusTrap function's API including focus management for modal dialogs, focus restoration on close, and tab order enforcement within trapped elements. Explain the focus trapping algorithm, focus boundary detection, and integration with the positioning system for modal interfaces. Include practical examples showing modal dialog focus management, drawer navigation, and overlay component focus handling. Document focus restoration patterns, accessibility compliance with WAI-ARIA standards, and integration with other interaction composables. Address edge cases for dynamic content, nested focus traps, and integration with form validation and input focus management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Keyboard Interaction Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VFloat's keyboard interaction utilities focusing on accessibility and user experience. Document the isUsingKeyboard utility for detecting keyboard navigation versus mouse interaction, enabling appropriate UI behavior switching. Explain the useActiveDescendant composable for managing ARIA active descendant patterns in complex UI components. Detail integration patterns with focus management, list navigation, and keyboard-driven interactions. Cover accessibility compliance requirements and WCAG guidelines implementation. Include practical examples of implementing keyboard-friendly dropdowns, autocomplete components, and navigation systems. Document best practices for handling keyboard-only users, screen reader compatibility, and cross-platform keyboard behavior consistency. Address common accessibility challenges and solutions for keyboard-driven interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for advanced VFloat features covering sophisticated positioning and interaction capabilities. Document the floating tree system for hierarchical floating element management including node coordination, mutually exclusive submenus, and parent-child relationships. Explain virtual elements concept and implementation for flexible positioning logic beyond traditional anchor/floating element pairs. Detail the safe polygon algorithm for smooth pointer navigation and hover interactions. Cover device pixel ratio awareness and visual quality improvements. Document keyboard interaction utilities including isUsingKeyboard detection and active descendant management. Include advanced integration patterns, performance optimization techniques, and complex use case implementations for nested menus, context menus, and sophisticated UI patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation covering all public interfaces and function signatures in VFloat. Document the complete exported API including all composables (useFloating, useHover, useClick, useFocus, useEscapeKey, useArrow, useListNavigation, useFloatingTree, useClientPoint, useFocusTrap), utility functions, and type definitions. For each composable, provide function signatures, parameter descriptions, return value specifications, and TypeScript interface definitions. Include configuration option schemas, default values, and validation rules. Document the FloatingContext structure, floatingStyles object, and all reactive properties. Cover middleware interfaces, virtual element contracts, and event callback signatures. Organize content for easy lookup and cross-referencing, with clear examples for each API endpoint and common usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Examples & Showcase
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive examples and showcase content demonstrating practical VFloat implementations. Document basic component examples including tooltip creation, dropdown menu setup, modal dialog implementation, and simple popover usage. Cover advanced examples showing complex menu systems, custom positioning logic, middleware combinations, and integration patterns. Include interactive playground components from the demo directory with detailed code explanations. Provide real-world use case implementations for common UI patterns like context menus, notification systems, and form controls. Document best practices for styling, accessibility, and performance optimization. Include comparison examples showing different approaches to similar problems and guidance on when to use specific composables and configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guide content covering contribution guidelines, development setup, and project maintenance procedures. Document the development environment setup including Node.js requirements, package manager preferences, and build tool configuration. Explain the project structure, coding standards enforced by Biome, and TypeScript configuration. Cover the testing strategy using Vitest with browser testing capabilities, unit test patterns, and component testing approaches. Detail the release process, version management, and continuous integration workflows. Include contribution guidelines, pull request procedures, and code review processes. Document the project governance, decision-making processes, and community interaction guidelines. Address debugging techniques, performance profiling, and troubleshooting common development issues.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# V-Float: A Comprehensive Vue 3 Floating UI Library

## Project Introduction

V-Float is a Vue 3 library that brings the power of Floating UI positioning algorithms to Vue applications. Built as a Vue 3 port of the popular Floating UI library, V-Float provides a flexible foundation for creating floating UI elements like tooltips, popovers, dropdowns, and modals.

### Purpose Statement
The library aims to solve the complex problem of positioning floating elements relative to anchor elements with precision, reliability, and ease of use. It abstracts away the complexity of collision detection, viewport boundaries, and responsive positioning while maintaining full control over the user experience.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Precision Positioning**: Provide pixel-perfect positioning with automatic collision detection
- **Vue 3 Integration**: Leverage Vue's Composition API for reactive, composable architecture
- **Cross-platform Support**: Work seamlessly across desktop, mobile, and touch devices
- **TypeScript First**: Full TypeScript support with comprehensive type definitions
- **Performance Optimized**: Lightweight implementation with tree-shaking support

### Target Audience
- Vue 3 developers building interactive UI components
- Design systems teams requiring consistent floating element behavior
- Accessibility-focused applications needing robust keyboard navigation
- Performance-conscious applications requiring efficient positioning calculations

## Technical Architecture

V-Float follows a modular, composable architecture built around Vue 3's Composition API. The system is organized into distinct layers that handle positioning, interactions, and middleware processing.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[useFloating] --> B[Positioning Engine]
    A --> C[Middleware Pipeline]
    A --> D[State Management]
    
    B --> E[Floating UI Core]
    C --> F[Offset Middleware]
    C --> G[Flip Middleware]
    C --> H[Shift Middleware]
    C --> I[Arrow Middleware]
    
    D --> J[Reactive State]
    D --> K[Auto Update]
    D --> L[Open State]
    
    M[Interaction Composables] --> A
    N[Focus Trap] --> A
    O[Virtual Elements] --> A
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of three primary layers:

1. **Positioning Layer**: Core positioning logic using Floating UI's algorithms
2. **Interaction Layer**: User interaction handlers (hover, click, focus, etc.)
3. **Middleware Layer**: Pluggable positioning modifiers

### Design Patterns

V-Float employs several key design patterns:

- **Composition Pattern**: Modular composables that can be combined
- **Observer Pattern**: Automatic updates on DOM changes
- **Strategy Pattern**: Configurable positioning strategies
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic middleware creation

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Anchor Element] --> B[useFloating Context]
    B --> C[Floating Element]
    B --> D[Middleware Pipeline]
    D --> E[Position Calculation]
    E --> F[Collision Detection]
    F --> G[Viewport Adjustment]
    G --> H[Final Position]
    H --> I[CSS Styles]
    I --> C
    
    J[Interaction Events] --> B
    K[Keyboard Input] --> B
    L[Touch Events] --> B
    M[Scroll Events] --> B
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The library exposes a clean API through its main entry point:

**Primary Export**: `src/index.ts`
- Re-exports all composables from the composables directory
- Provides a unified interface for library consumers

**Core Composable**: `src/composables/positioning/use-floating.ts`
- Implements the main positioning logic
- Handles anchor/floating element relationships
- Manages reactive state and updates

### Core Modules

**Positioning System**: `src/composables/positioning/`
- `use-floating.ts`: Core positioning logic with middleware support
- `use-arrow.ts`: Arrow positioning for tooltips and popovers
- `use-client-point.ts`: Cursor/touch coordinate positioning

**Interaction System**: `src/composables/interactions/`
- `use-hover.ts`: Mouse hover interactions with delays and safe polygons
- `use-click.ts`: Click event handling with outside click detection
- `use-focus.ts`: Keyboard focus management
- `use-escape-key.ts`: Escape key handling
- `use-list-navigation.ts`: Keyboard navigation for lists

**Middleware System**: `src/composables/middlewares/`
- `arrow.ts`: Arrow positioning middleware
- Extensible middleware architecture for custom positioning logic

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration**: `vite.config.ts`
- Library mode configuration for multiple output formats
- Vue plugin integration with UnoCSS styling
- TypeScript declaration generation

**Type Definitions**: `src/types.ts`
- Comprehensive type definitions for all public APIs
- Virtual element interfaces for flexible positioning
- Open state change reason types for event tracking

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- `@floating-ui/dom`: Core positioning algorithms
- `@vueuse/core`: Vue composition utilities
- `vue`: Vue 3 runtime and compiler

**Development Dependencies**:
- `vite`: Build tool and development server
- `vitest`: Testing framework with browser support
- `vitepress`: Documentation site generator
- `unocss`: Utility-first CSS framework

### Integration Points

**Vue 3 Integration**: Seamless integration with Vue's reactivity system
**TypeScript Integration**: Full type safety throughout the API
**CSS Framework Integration**: Compatible with Tailwind, UnoCSS, and custom styles
**Testing Integration**: Comprehensive test coverage with Vitest

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[useFloating] --> B[useHover]
    A --> C[useClick]
    A --> D[useFocus]
    A --> E[useEscapeKey]
    
    A --> F[useArrow]
    A --> G[offset]
    A --> H[flip]
    A --> I[shift]
    
    B --> J[safePolygon]
    C --> K[outsideClick]
    D --> L[keyboardEvents]
    E --> M[escapeKeyEvents]
    
    F --> N[Arrow Styles]
    G --> O[Position Offset]
    H --> P[Placement Flipping]
    I --> Q[Viewport Shifting]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

V-Float provides a comprehensive solution for floating element positioning with the following core capabilities:

**Advanced Positioning Algorithms**:
- Automatic collision detection and viewport boundary handling
- Smart placement flipping and shifting
- Precise coordinate calculation with device pixel ratio consideration
- Support for virtual elements and custom positioning logic

**Rich Interaction System**:
- Hover interactions with configurable delays and safe polygons
- Click-to-toggle behavior with outside click detection
- Keyboard navigation and accessibility support
- Touch and mobile device compatibility

**Flexible Middleware Architecture**:
- Pluggable positioning modifiers
- Custom middleware development support
- Chainable middleware operations
- Performance-optimized execution pipeline

### Implementation Highlights

**Core Positioning Logic**: `src/composables/positioning/use-floating.ts`
- Implements Floating UI's core positioning algorithms
- Handles reactive state management and updates
- Supports transform-based and top/left positioning modes
- Automatic cleanup of event listeners and observers

**Advanced Hover Interactions**: `src/composables/interactions/use-hover.ts`
- Sophisticated pointer enter/leave detection
- Configurable delay mechanisms for open/close actions
- Safe polygon algorithm for smooth pointer navigation
- Mouse-only mode for desktop optimization

**Arrow Positioning**: `src/composables/middlewares/arrow.ts`
- Automatic arrow element positioning
- Padding and offset configuration
- Integration with the middleware pipeline
- Device pixel ratio aware calculations

**Virtual Element Support**: `src/types.ts`
- Flexible anchor element definition
- Custom positioning logic through virtual elements
- Context element support for layout calculations
- Extensible interface for advanced use cases

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unmounted
    Unmounted --> Idle: mount
    Idle --> Opening: hover/click/focus
    Opening --> Open: position calculated
    Open --> Closing: leave/blur/escape
    Closing --> Idle: closed
    Open --> Open: position update
    Idle --> Unmounted: unmount
    
    Opening --> Error: positioning failed
    Error --> Idle: retry
```

**State Management Flow**:
- Reactive state tracking through Vue's reactivity system
- Automatic positioning updates on state changes
- Cleanup of resources on component unmount
- Error handling and recovery mechanisms

**Middleware Execution Flow**:
- Sequential middleware processing pipeline
- Early termination support for optimization
- Data sharing between middleware stages
- Custom middleware registration and execution

**Interaction Event Flow**:
- Event delegation and propagation handling
- Debouncing and throttling for performance
- Cross-device event normalization
- Accessibility-compliant event handling

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [src/index.ts](src/index.ts)
- [src/composables/index.ts](src/composables/index.ts)
- [src/composables/positioning/use-floating.ts](src/composables/positioning/use-floating.ts)
- [src/composables/middlewares/arrow.ts](src/composables/middlewares/arrow.ts)
- [src/composables/interactions/use-hover.ts](src/composables/interactions/use-hover.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [playground/main.ts](playground/main.ts)
- [playground/App.vue](playground/App.vue)
- [src/types.ts](src/types.ts)
- [docs/guide/concepts.md](docs/guide/concepts.md)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [playground/demo/Tooltip.vue](playground/demo/Tooltip.vue)

</blog>
